title: Finding Your Voice Again with These 10 Herbal Teas for a Lost Voice
intro_desc: Herbal teas for lost voice are natural remedies that help alleviate vocal cord inflammation and irritation, allowing individuals to regain their lost voice. The best herbal teas for lost voice include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Rosmarinus officinalis, Verbascum thapsus, Trifolium pratense, Ginkgo biloba, Lavandula angustifolia, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, lost voice can severely impact people's lives, causing difficulties in communication, affecting relationships, and even leading to missed work or school opportunities, resulting in significant emotional and financial strain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a lost voice. The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, allowing the vocal cords to heal and function properly. When we have a lost voice, it's often due to strain or inflammation in the throat, and glycyrrhizin can help alleviate these issues. Drinking licorice root tea can also help calm the throat and promote a soothing, gentle healing process. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it easier for the vocal cords to recover. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, licorice root tea can help get your voice back to normal.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin helps with lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and allowing the voice to heal naturally.
Polyphenols are good for lost voice because they improve blood flow to the vocal cords, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing of damaged tissues.
Flavonoids are good for lost voice because they help improve blood flow to the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and promoting healing, which can help restore a person's ability to speak clearly.
Saponins are good for lost voice because they help reduce inflammation in the vocal cords, promote healing, and improve blood flow to the affected area, thereby restoring voice quality.
Tannins are good for a lost voice because they help improve blood flow to the vocal cords, which can reduce swelling and promote healing, allowing the voice to return to its normal sound and pitch.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with lost voice, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, and a pot of boiling water. Begin by measuring out one teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves for every cup of water you plan to use. Place the leaves in the tea infuser or muslin bag, and then steep them in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. The longer steeping time will help to release more of the beneficial compounds from the Ginkgo biloba leaves. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the tea infuser or muslin bag from the pot, and then pour the tea into a cup. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to wait until the tea has cooled down a bit before adding any sweetener or citrus. Finally, drink the tea while it is still warm to help soothe and restore your lost voice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe a lost voice. The leaves of the thyme plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the vocal cords. When the vocal cords are swollen, they can't vibrate properly, leading to a hoarse or lost voice. Drinking thymus vulgaris tea can help calm the vocal cords and reduce inflammation, allowing them to heal and function properly again. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help protect the vocal cords from damage and promote healing. Additionally, thymus vulgaris tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections that can cause a lost voice. By drinking thymus vulgaris tea, you can help your vocal cords recover and get your voice back to normal.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for a lost voice because it helps to reduce inflammation in the vocal cords and protect them from damage, promoting a faster recovery of a person's normal speaking voice.
Rosmarinic acid is good for a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for a more normal and clear voice.
Carvacrol is good for a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the vocal cords, making it easier to speak and regain a strong, clear voice.
Tannins are good for a lost voice because they help reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, which can become irritated and swollen when we lose our voice.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that can help with a lost voice, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Start by boiling water in a kettle or on the stove, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Place one teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves into the tea infuser or directly into the heat-resistant cup. Pour the simmering water over the leaves, making sure they are fully submerged. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavor is strong. Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the leaves. Ginkgo biloba tea can be consumed hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. To help with a lost voice, drink the tea 2-3 times a day for several days, and it is recommended to combine it with other herbal teas, such as licorice root or slippery elm, to help soothe and protect the vocal cords.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a good remedy for a lost voice because it helps to soothe and calm the throat. The tea contains natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to speak. This plant-based remedy is also rich in antioxidants, which protect the throat from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has antimicrobial properties that fight off infections and bacteria that can cause a sore throat. By drinking Echinacea purpurea tea, you can help to reduce inflammation, calm the throat, and promote healing, allowing your voice to recover faster. Echinacea purpurea tea is also easy to make and can be consumed hot or cold, making it a convenient and accessible remedy for a lost voice.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for lost voice because they help to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation in the throat, allowing the vocal cords to recover and regain their normal function.
Polyphenols are good for a lost voice because they help to improve blood flow to the vocal cords and reduce inflammation, which can help to restore a person's ability to speak clearly and loudly.
Cichoric acid is good for a lost voice because it helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, allowing for better voice production and recovery.
Quercetin is good for lost voice because it has powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and protect the vocal cords from damage.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that can help with a lost voice, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a teapot, a cup, and a strainer. Begin by boiling water in the teapot, then add one teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 ounces of water. Let the leaves steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their beneficial properties. Next, use the strainer to carefully pour the tea into the cup, straining out the leaves. Finally, drink the tea while it's still warm, ideally before singing or using your voice. Some people find it helpful to drink this tea a few times a day to help soothe and restore their vocal cords. It's essential to note that Ginkgo biloba is not a substitute for medical treatment, and if your lost voice persists or is accompanied by other concerning symptoms, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper guidance and care.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, can be helpful when you have a lost voice. The main reason is that ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords. When you are sick or have an illness, your throat can become sore and inflamed, making it hard to talk or sing. Ginger tea can help to ease this discomfort and reduce the swelling, making it easier to speak. Ginger also has natural soothing properties that can calm the throat and vocal cords, helping to reduce strain and irritation. Additionally, ginger has antibacterial properties that can help fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the lost voice. Drinking ginger tea can provide quick relief and help you recover your voice faster.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for a lost voice because they help reduce inflammation in the throat and improve blood flow to the vocal cords, allowing for a faster recovery and a more stable voice.
Gingerols are good for a lost voice because they help to reduce inflammation and fight off the viruses that cause hoarseness and throat irritation.
Shogaols are good for lost voice because they help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, which can cause a sore or hoarse voice, by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Gingerol helps to heal a lost voice because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the vocal cords, allowing for a faster recovery and return to normal voice.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with a lost voice, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and fresh ginger root. Begin by rinsing the ginger root under cold water, then peel it using a vegetable peeler. Slice the peeled ginger into thin pieces and place them in a saucepan. Next, add one teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the saucepan and pour in two cups of boiling water. Cover the saucepan and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their healing properties. After the steeping time is up, strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey to taste, but be sure to let the tea cool down before consuming it. Drink the Ginkgo biloba herbal tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and restore your voice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is often used to help people with a sore or lost voice. This is because eucalyptus has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat. When we have a sore throat, it can be hard to talk or sing because the throat is swollen and painful. Eucalyptus globulus tea can help to calm the throat and make it easier to speak. It also has antiseptic properties that can help to fight off any infections that might be causing the sore throat. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea can help to soothe the throat, reduce pain and discomfort, and get the voice back to normal. The steam from the tea can also help to loosen mucus in the throat and make it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful if you have a cold or flu. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, eucalyptus globulus tea can help to get your voice back and make it easier to communicate.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for lost voice because it helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, which can cause a hoarse or lost voice.
Catechins are good for lost voice because they help increase blood flow to the vocal cords, allowing for better oxygenation and reducing inflammation that can cause hoarseness.
Tannins are good for lost voice because they help to reduce inflammation in the throat and improve blood flow to the vocal cords, allowing the voice to heal and recover more quickly.
Eucalyptol is good for a lost voice because it helps to break down thick mucus in the throat and calm inflamed tissues, allowing for a smoother and easier recovery of one's voice.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that can help with a lost voice, start by gathering dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy these at a local health food store or online. Next, boil a cup of water and add one teaspoon of the dried leaves to it. Let it steep for about 5-7 minutes, or until the water has cooled down slightly. You can then strain the tea and discard the leaves. To add some sweetness, you can mix in a bit of honey or sugar. To help soothe your voice, you can also add a teaspoon of licorice root or slippery elm to the tea. Finally, drink the tea while it's still warm and try to rest your voice as much as possible. Drinking this tea a few times a day may help to alleviate your lost voice and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary tea, is a herbal remedy that can help soothe a lost voice. This is because rosemary has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to speak and swallow. When you have a sore throat, it can be painful to talk or even eat, but rosemary tea can help calm the area and reduce discomfort. The antioxidants in rosemary also help to fight off infections, which can cause a sore throat and a lost voice in the first place. Additionally, rosemary tea can help to loosen mucus and promote drainage, allowing the throat to heal faster. Drinking rosemary tea regularly may also help to improve circulation and reduce stress, which can contribute to a lost voice.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the vocal cords and improve circulation to help restore a healthy and strong voice.
Carnosic acid is good for a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the vocal cords and promote healing.
Polyphenols are good for lost voice because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the vocal cords, which can aid in healing and restoring normal voice function.
Carvacrol is good for lost voice because it helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat, allowing the vocal cords to heal and recover their normal function.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with a lost voice, start by gathering Ginkgo biloba leaves and a few other herbs like licorice root and thyme that have soothing properties. Begin by rinsing the Ginkgo biloba leaves in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of the Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 teaspoon of licorice root and a few sprigs of thyme in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to release. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add a bit of honey to sweeten the tea if you prefer. Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal your vocal cords, and to get your voice back to normal. Note: Consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tea, also known as mullein tea, has been used for centuries to help soothe a sore or lost voice. This is because the plant contains a compound called saponin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink mullein tea, the saponin helps to reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords. This makes it easier to speak and reduces the discomfort associated with a sore voice. The tea also has a soothing effect on the throat, which can help to calm irritation and inflammation. Additionally, mullein tea is a natural expectorant, which means it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, further reducing congestion and discomfort. The warm liquid also helps to moisturize the throat, making it feel more comfortable and allowing the voice to return to normal.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for a lost voice because they help to improve blood flow to the vocal cords, reducing swelling and inflammation, and promoting healing and recovery of the vocal cords.
Tannins are good for lost voice because they help to reduce inflammation in the throat and improve blood flow to the vocal cords, allowing for smoother and more efficient sound production.

Flavonoids are good for lost voice because they have antioxidant properties that help protect the vocal cords from damage and promote blood flow to the throat area, reducing inflammation and allowing the voice to recover.

remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea for a lost voice, start by gathering 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root, which is known for soothing the throat. Then, boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for a minute. Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves and licorice root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour the cooled water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and nutrients to mix. Strain the tea into a cup and add honey to taste, if desired. After drinking the tea, gargle with warm water to help soothe the throat and promote healing. It's essential to note that Ginkgo biloba tea should not be consumed in excess, as it can cause stomach upset. Drinking 1 cup of this herbal tea, 2-3 times a day, may help to alleviate a lost voice and promote vocal cord health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense tea, also known as red clover tea, is made from the flowers of the red clover plant. It's a good remedy for a lost voice because the plant contains isoflavones, which are natural compounds that can help soothe and calm the throat. When you have a lost voice, your throat can become inflamed and sore, making it hard to speak. The isoflavones in Trifolium pratense tea can help reduce inflammation and calm the throat, making it easier to speak. Additionally, the tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the vocal cords from damage. Drinking Trifolium pratense tea regularly may also help to improve the overall health of the throat and vocal cords, which can help prevent a lost voice in the future. This can be especially helpful for people who use their voice a lot, such as singers, public speakers, or teachers.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin in Ginkgo Biloba helps to soothe a lost voice by reducing inflammation and swelling in the vocal cords, allowing for a smooth and clear recovery.
Kaempferol is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect the vocal cords from damage and irritation, promoting a quick recovery of the voice.
Tannins are good for lost voice because they help to reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for faster healing and a more rapid return of normal voice function.
Isorhapontigenin is good for lost voice because it helps to reduce inflammation and protect the vocal cords, allowing the voice to heal and recover faster.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with a lost voice, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water. Place the dried leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup, and pour the boiling water over them. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their active ingredients. Strain the tea into a separate cup to remove the leaves, and add honey to taste if you prefer it sweet. Once the tea has cooled down a bit, gargle with it several times to help soothe your throat and promote blood flow to your vocal cords. Repeat this process a few times a day to help recover your lost voice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is often used to soothe a lost voice. The tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which contain powerful compounds that help to calm and relax the vocal cords. When we lose our voice, it's often due to inflammation or irritation of the vocal cords, which can cause pain and discomfort. Lavandula angustifolia tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce this inflammation and calm the vocal cords. This allows the vocal cords to heal and recover more quickly, which can help to restore our voice. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of the tea can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can further contribute to vocal strain. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea regularly may also help to prevent future vocal strain and promote overall vocal health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, allowing the vocal cords to heal and recover their normal function.
Linalyl acetate is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the vocal cords and improve vocal function.
Apigenin is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the vocal cords, promoting faster recovery and soothing a sore throat.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with lost voice, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh leaves. You can purchase these leaves from a health food store or online. Next, boil a cup of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Then, add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can also add a tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if you prefer. Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and promote healing in the throat, while its antioxidant properties can help soothe and calm the vocal cords. Drinking this tea 2-3 times a day may help you regain your lost voice and feel better.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a natural remedy that can help with a lost voice. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. When you have a lost voice, the vocal cords are often swollen and inflamed, making it hard to speak. Ginkgo biloba tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the vocal cords. It also helps to improve blood flow to the throat, which brings oxygen and nutrients to the damaged area, promoting healing and recovery. As a result, the vocal cords can start to function normally again, allowing you to speak clearly and easily. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea regularly may also help to prevent future episodes of a lost voice by keeping the vocal cords healthy and strong.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin helps to soothe a lost voice by reducing inflammation and fighting off free radicals that can cause damage to the vocal cords.
Kaempferol is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the vocal cords, allowing for faster recovery and improved voice quality.
Isorhapontigenin helps to recover a lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the vocal cords, making it easier to speak and vocalize.

Bilobalide is good for lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the larynx and vocal cords, allowing for a smoother and more efficient flow of air to help restore voice.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for lost voice
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tea that helps with a lost voice, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a tea infuser. Begin by boiling one cup of water in a pot, then reduce the heat to low. Next, add a teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser and place it in the pot. Allow the leaves to steep for about 5-7 minutes, depending on how strong you prefer the flavor. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the tea infuser and discard the leaves. Strain the tea into a cup to ensure it's free of any loose leaves. Finally, add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the tea while it's still warm to help soothe your throat and potentially restore your lost voice. It's essential to note that Ginkgo biloba tea may not work for everyone and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before trying any new herbal remedies.

supplementary_best: A combination of herbal teas made from 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (licorice root), 'Thymus vulgaris' (thyme), and 'Rosmarinus officinalis' (rosemary) can help remedy a lost voice. Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce swelling, making it easier to speak. Thymus vulgaris contains compounds that have antimicrobial properties, helping to fight off infections that may be causing the lost voice. Rosmarinus officinalis, rich in antioxidants, protects the vocal cords from damage and promotes healing. This combination works well because the anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra reduce swelling, the antimicrobial properties of Thymus vulgaris fight off infections, and the antioxidant properties of Rosmarinus officinalis promote healing and protect the vocal cords. Drinking a tea made from these three herbs can help restore a person's voice by addressing the underlying causes of a lost voice.
supplementary_avoid: If you have lost your voice, you should avoid certain herbal teas that can irritate your throat or make your hoarseness worse. One such tea is Sage (Salvia officinalis). It can dry out your throat and make it harder to speak. Another tea to avoid is Yerba Mate (Ilex paraguariensis). It is a stimulant and can strain your vocal cords, making it harder to recover from a lost voice. Cloves (Syzygium aromaticum) are also not recommended as they contain strong oils that can irritate your throat. Additionally, Peppermint (Mentha piperita) and Spearmint (Mentha spicata) teas should be avoided as they can be too cooling and irritating for a sore throat. Finally, Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia) and Marjoram (Origanum majorana) teas are best avoided as well, as they can be too strong and drying for a lost voice.
supplementary_other_remedies: Gargling with salt water is a natural remedy that can help soothe a lost voice. When you gargle with warm salt water, it reduces swelling in the throat, making it easier to speak and sing. This is because salt helps to draw out excess fluids and reduce inflammation. You can make a salt water solution by mixing 1/2 teaspoon of salt with 8 ounces of warm water. Some people also use honey to help soothe their lost voice. Honey has antibacterial properties that help to fight off infections, which can cause a lost voice. When you mix honey with warm water or warm milk, it creates a soothing mixture that can help calm your throat. Another remedy is to drink warm liquids, such as soup or broth, which can help to keep your vocal cords moist. This is especially helpful if you have a dry, scratchy throat. Finally, staying hydrated by drinking plenty of water can also help to keep your vocal cords healthy and functioning properly. When you are well-hydrated, your body is better able to fight off infections and heal itself.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have lost their voice, known as laryngitis, often experience other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common problem is a sore throat, which can be relieved with Slippery Elm bark. Its mucilaginous properties help soothe and protect the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.  Another issue is swollen lymph nodes in the neck, which can be alleviated with Echinacea. This herb boosts the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that cause swelling. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and discomfort in the affected area.  Dry coughs often accompany laryngitis, and herbs like Licorice Root can help. Its expectorant properties help loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to cough up and clear the airways.  Tension and stress can also contribute to laryngitis, and herbs like Passionflower can help. Its calming properties help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to recover from the illness.  Ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory, can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and neck. Its warming properties also help increase circulation, promoting healing and recovery.
